name: cors-scanner
summary:
CORS Scanner
A multi-threaded scanner that helps identify CORS flaws/misconfigurations

documentation.readme: CORS Scanner A multi-threaded scanner that helps identify CORS flaws/misconfigurations

arguments.name: -a
arguments.description: specify a domain, and value delimited with ~~~. Example: -a

arguments.name: --auth
arguments.description: specify a domain, and value delimited with ~~~. Example: -a

arguments.name: -c
arguments.description: specify a domain(s) and cookie(s) data delimited with ~~~. Example: -c

arguments.name: -ch
arguments.description: specify a custom header and value, delimited with ~~~. Example: -a

arguments.name: --cookies
arguments.description: specify a domain(s) and cookie(s) data delimited with ~~~. Example: -c

arguments.name: -csv
arguments.description: specify a CSV file name. If the file already exists, the file will be appended to.

arguments.name: --csv_name
arguments.description: specify a CSV file name. If the file already exists, the file will be appended to.

arguments.name: --custom-header
arguments.description: specify a custom header and value, delimited with ~~~. Example: -a

arguments.name: -pr
arguments.description: specify a proxy to use (-pr 127.0.0.1:8080)

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: specify a proxy to use (-pr 127.0.0.1:8080)

arguments.name: -r
arguments.description: specify a referer string to use.

arguments.name: --referer
arguments.description: specify a referer string to use.

arguments.name: -t
arguments.description: specify number of threads (default=10)

arguments.name: --threads
arguments.description: specify number of threads (default=10)

arguments.name: --timeout
arguments.description: specify number of seconds until a connection timeout (default=10)

arguments.name: -to
arguments.description: specify number of seconds until a connection timeout (default=10)

arguments.name: -u
arguments.description: specify a single url formatted http(s)://addr:port.

arguments.name: -ua
arguments.description: specify a User Agent string to use. Default is a random User Agent string.

arguments.name: -uf
arguments.description: specify a file containing urls formatted http(s)://addr:port.

arguments.name: --url
arguments.description: specify a single url formatted http(s)://addr:port.

arguments.name: --url_file
arguments.description: specify a file containing urls formatted http(s)://addr:port.

arguments.name: --useragent
arguments.description: specify a User Agent string to use. Default is a random User Agent string.

arguments.name: -v
arguments.description: increase output verbosity

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: increase output verbosity

documentation.how_to_use: Clone or install `cors-scanner` from its upstream repository, then run `cors-scanner --help` (or the README entrypoint) to confirm flags. CORS Scanner A multi-threaded scanner that helps identify CORS flaws/misconfigurations.
documentation.when_to_use:
Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [shell_linux] in the Penetration Testing category. Fits when the engagement needs: CORS Scanner
A multi-threaded scanner that helps identify CORS flaws/misconfigurations.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: CORS Scanner A multi-threaded scanner that helps identify CORS flaws/misconfigurations.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases:
When you need Penetration Testing, use cors-scanner because CORS Scanner
A multi-threaded scanner that helps identify CORS flaws/misconfigurations.
When chaining Penetration Testing tools, pair cors-scanner with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `cors-scanner --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
